Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 107. How Do You Feed Your Horses? (ABL 0097)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
o 1o 1

a-na LUGAL EN-ia

(1) To the king, my lord: your servant Ṭab-ṣill-Ešarra. Good health to the king, my lord! May Aššur and Mullissu bless the king, my lord!

o 22

ARAD-ka mDÙG.GA—ṣil—É.[ŠÁR.RA]

o 33

lu DI-mu a-na LUGAL EN-[ia]

o 44

aš-šur dNIN.LÍL a-na LUGAL

o 55

EN-ia lik-ru-bu

o 66

ša LUGAL EN ⸢iš⸣-pur-an-ni

(6) As to what the king, my lord, wrote to me: "Suppose you are staying at home, how do you feed (your horses)?"

o 77

ma-a i—su-ri* ina É-ka-ma

o 88

kam-mu-sa-ka ma-a mì-nu tú-šá-kal

o 99

meš-li ANŠE.KUR.RA-MEŠ-⸢ia*⸣

(9) Half of my horses are fed in the Barhalza province, the other half eat at the [... of the ci]ty of Issete in the domestic quarters of [...]; my cavalry [and ... are sta]tioned in the tax-collection center of the province of [...]. Their [......

o 1010

ina KUR.bar-ḫal-⸢zi* e*-ku*⸣-lu*

o 1111

meš-lu-ma ⸢ina⸣ [x x x] ⸢URU⸣.01-te

o 1212

[ina] É—02-⸢i⸣ [x x x] e-ku-lu

o 1313

⸢ANŠE⸣.BAD-ḪAL-⸢li⸣ [x x]-MEŠ-ni

o 1414

[ina] ⸢pi*⸣-ri KUR*.[x x] ⸢kar*⸣-ru

o 1515

[x x]-⸢šú*⸣-nu [x x x x]+⸢x⸣-qa-na

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P334046/.
